## Mode proof

The active branch is `feature/evidence-closeout-pilot`, which resolves to `RESEARCH` under `docs/governance_mode.md`.

For this task, `RESEARCH` allows a tracked, reproducible diagnostics note that reduces evidence drift and clarifies branch-specific risks. It does not authorize runtime/default changes, paper/live work, promotion/readiness/champion claims, family-rule changes, or changes under strict-only surfaces.

This premortem would require reclassification or separate governance if it became a plan to touch any of the following:

- `config/strategy/champions/`
- `.github/workflows/champion-freeze-guard.yml`
- `src/core/strategy/family_registry.py`
- `src/core/strategy/family_admission.py`
- runtime-default authority surfaces
- comparison, readiness, promotion, champion, or family-rule surfaces
- paper/live execution edges

## Premortem frame

Assume it is `2026-11-15` and `feature/evidence-closeout-pilot` failed to improve Genesis-Core. The failure was not that the branch introduced a large runtime regression. The sharper failure was that a branch meant to reduce evidence drift became another layer of partially remembered proof.

The branch had the right direction: identify decision-influencing artifacts, choose `execution_proxy_evidence` as the first replay-smoke candidate, avoid ignored `results/research/**` inputs, select a tracked minimal fixture, and keep broader replay-framework work out of scope. The branch then started converting that into a focused smoke implementation.

Six months later, the failure pattern is that the branch's strongest local win was overgeneralized:

1. one minimal fixture proved one script-level replay path,
2. that proof was later remembered as a clean-checkout guarantee for the wider evidence system,
3. broader ignored-artifact chains stayed outside commit-safe replay,
4. RI/policy-router research continued to accumulate exact-window observations faster than falsifier gates matured,
5. paper/live and promotion-adjacent conversations inherited confidence from evidence-closeout notes,
6. the repo became more internally convincing without becoming proportionally more operationally safe.

The branch therefore fails if it lowers friction for true claims but raises the chance that narrow claims are cited outside their envelope.

## Ranked failure modes

| Rank | Failure mode                                             | Likelihood  | Impact      | Branch-specific mechanism                                                                                                                                                                                   | Early warning signal                                                                                                                  |
| ---: | -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|    1 | Replay-smoke overclaim                                   | High        | Very high   | The tracked `execution_proxy_evidence` fixture smoke proves one compact replay path, but later workers treat it as clean-checkout replay coverage for broader ignored historical artifacts or other chains. | A note says "clean-checkout replay is solved" without naming the exact fixture, script, inputs, and excluded chains.                  |
|    2 | Evidence-to-authority drift                              | High        | Very high   | `docs-only`, `observational`, and `non-authorizing` packets become operational shorthand for approval once they are cited often enough.                                                                     | A runtime/config/promotion packet cites a diagnostics note as if it granted implementation authority.                                 |
|    3 | RI/policy-router local-pocket overfitting                | High        | High        | Exact-subject and exact-window evidence remains compelling but mixed, with transport failures and context leakage; a future candidate may skip a fresh falsifier gate.                                      | A rule is argued from one month/window or frozen bank without contradiction-year, external-surface, or transport-failure handling.    |
|    4 | Clean-checkout illusion from fixture minimization        | Medium-high | High        | A minimal tracked fixture is useful for smoke coverage, but may not preserve the full historical trace semantics needed for stronger replay attestation.                                                    | The fixture becomes the only reproduced input while original ignored artifacts remain untracked, unhashed, or unavailable.            |
|    5 | Queue drift after completed slices                       | Medium-high | Medium-high | The `next_phase_verkstad_queue` intentionally advances one bounded risk at a time, but as slices complete, older packets may still look like live instructions.                                             | Workers follow the stale "next" line from an older packet instead of the current queue status.                                        |

|    9 | Test coverage narrows around the happy fixture           | Medium      | High        | Focused smoke tests can become strong around the new tracked fixture while missing malformed trace, missing field, non-finite, duplicate key, or historical-shape cases.                                    | Coverage grows only around the minimal fixture and not around failure-mode assertions.                                                |
|   10 | Ignored artifact dependency persists                     | Medium      | High        | The branch reduces one ignored-input dependency, but many decision-influencing chains may still depend on ignored `results/**` material.                                                                    | A later analysis can run only on the original workstation because required inputs are ignored or locally generated.                   |

Fixture smoke does not cover all of that.                             | A result omits env flags, cache read/write posture, symbol mode, data-source policy, warmup, or config hash.                          |
|   12 | Promotion language creeps into research notes            | Medium      | Very high   | Reproducibility improvements can make evidence feel more promotion-ready than it is, especially around candidate lifecycle and RI policy-router findings.                                                   | Phrases like "ready", "candidate winner", "safe default", or "champion path" appear before promotion-compare authority exists.        |
|   13 | Documentation topology becomes the control plane         | Medium      | Medium-high | The repo uses many packets, diagnostics, queues, working contracts, and governance references; navigation errors become execution errors.                                                                   | A worker cannot identify the current active lane without reading multiple historical documents in conflict.                           |
|   14 | Security/API operational hygiene is treated as unrelated | Low-medium  | High        | Because this branch is evidence-focused, bearer auth, raw API errors, `.env`, and paper-order safety can be mentally separated from replay assurance.                                                       | Local secrets, raw exception text, or auth bypass assumptions appear in diagnostics or test scaffolding.                              |

## Branch-specific failure chain

The most plausible failure chain is:

1. **Correct local containment:** The branch lands one tracked fixture and focused tests for `execution_proxy_evidence`.
2. **Vocabulary compression:** "tracked-fixture replay smoke" becomes "clean-checkout replay".
3. **Scope inheritance:** Other artifact chains such as `edge_origin_isolation` or broader RI replay surfaces inherit the confidence without their own carriers.
4. **Authority migration:** Observational evidence notes become convenient citations in runtime, config, paper, or promotion discussions.
5. **Research acceleration:** Exact-window RI/policy-router findings continue to accumulate faster than falsifier gates and transport checks are made mandatory for candidate promotion.
6. **Operational misread:** A later paper/live or champion-adjacent decision treats reproducible research evidence as operational safety evidence.
7. **Late discovery:** The mismatch appears only when a clean checkout, another machine, or an operational dry-run cannot reproduce the same evidence envelope.

The branch succeeds only if step 2 is prevented. The exact phrase must remain: one bounded smoke, for one script, with one tracked fixture, under one branch context.

## Subsystem risk analysis

### Evidence closeout and replay smoke

The branch has moved the evidence-closeout line in the right direction: from ignored local results toward commit-safe reproducibility. The current queue records that the fourth slice has landed a minimal tracked fixture and focused smoke coverage for `execution_proxy_evidence`.

The residual risk is precision loss. A smoke test is not the same as full replay attestation. A minimal fixture is not the same as the historical trace. A deterministic output from a compact payload is not the same as proof that all decision-influencing evidence can be regenerated from tracked inputs.

Preventive stance:

- always name the exact replay subject, input carrier, script, and test selector;
- keep `execution_proxy_evidence` separate from `edge_origin_isolation`, SCPE replay, and broader artifact chains;
- record whether a claim is fixture-level, historical-trace-level, or clean-checkout full-chain-level;
- require separate carrier decisions for every decision-influencing artifact chain.

### Governance and evidence authority

The repo has strong governance language, but volume is itself a hazard. Packets, closeouts, queue notes, working contracts, and diagnostics all contain correct caveats. The risk is not absence of caveats; it is that later work remembers the conclusion and drops the caveat.

On this branch, the dangerous shortcut is:

- "fixture containment selected" becomes "replay problem solved";
- "smoke implementation landed" becomes "evidence chain is portable";
- "premortem risk reduced" becomes "future stricter gates are unnecessary".

Preventive stance:

- every new diagnostics or decision note should carry a short "does not authorize" block;
- queue updates should explicitly mark completed slices as historical rather than live next steps;
- evidence notes should separate observed, inferred, and unverified claims.

### RI/policy-router research quality

The current working contract shows an active RI/policy-router research context with exact-subject findings, mixed signs, parked lines, and explicit non-authority caveats. This is scientifically healthier than a single winner narrative, but it remains fragile.

The branch-specific risk is that replay/evidence-closeout improvements make local findings feel more portable. They do not. A reproducible exact-window finding can still be exact-window-only, context-leaky, or falsified on transport.

Preventive stance:

- keep local-pocket findings as hypothesis generators unless a fresh packet opens transport/falsifier work;
- require at least one contradiction surface before any runtime-candidate language;
- make negative evidence first-class, especially when unchanged transport fails;
- keep policy-router research distinct from family-rule, promotion, and champion surfaces.

### Paper/live boundary

Paper/live risk is low-frequency and high-impact. This branch does not touch paper/live surfaces, but that does not make paper/live irrelevant. The danger is semantic inheritance: replay smoke and manifest discipline can be mistaken for operational safety.

Paper/live safety requires different proof: dry-run behavior, state idempotence, order-submission isolation, auth, corrupt-state handling, exchange/API failure paths, and explicit live-paper acceptance. None of those are granted by a diagnostics note, fixture, or replay smoke.

Preventive stance:

- never cite this premortem, fixture smoke, or evidence-closeout work as paper/live readiness;
- require a separate paper-shadow or live-paper seam check before work approaches execution edges;
- keep `--live-paper` style surfaces incompatible with experimental observability unless separately reviewed.

### Config and runtime authority

Earlier premortem follow-up work reduced runtime-config ambiguity, but the branch can still regress conceptually. Config schema validation, config propose authority, runtime defaults, replay fixtures, and evidence manifests are different proof domains.

The failure mode is a side-door argument: because a config-bearing result is reproducible, the underlying config path is treated as safe to update live. That is invalid without config-authority review.

Preventive stance:

- keep validate/propose/live-update matrices separate from replay evidence;
- do not change runtime authority as a side effect of research reproducibility;
- require explicit tests for validate/propose behavior if a future slice touches config authority.

### Backtest, optimizer, cache, and environment

Genesis-Core depends on deterministic envelopes: code SHA, runtime config, candidate config, data source, symbol mode, warmup, env flags, canonical fast/precompute mode, feature cache policy, and artifact paths. The branch narrows one replay carrier, but does not eliminate envelope risk.

The branch could fail if future workers use the tracked fixture as a substitute for documenting the whole envelope. A fixture-level smoke can confirm a script's deterministic behavior without proving that full backtest/optimizer runs are comparable across environments.

Preventive stance:

- require claim headers for decision-influencing evidence;
- state cache read/write posture, especially when generated caches are suppressed or ignored;
- separate fixture-level tests from full historical replay, optimizer, and champion comparison evidence.

### Agent and customization surfaces

The branch already treated editor-worker customization drift as a first-class operational risk. That remains correct. Genesis-Core has repo governance, local Claude support surfaces, Codex instructions, GitHub agents, registry metadata, and working-contract anchors. Any one of these can become stale.

The branch fails if future workers use a convenient older instruction file rather than resolving current mode, current branch, current queue, and actual touched surfaces.

Preventive stance:

- dispatch workers with explicit scope, write set, base SHA, and forbidden inheritance boundaries;
- stop on stale base SHA or overlapping write sets;
- treat `GENESIS_WORKING_CONTRACT.md` as a drift anchor, not SSOT;
- keep current queue status more authoritative than stale historical "next step" prose.

### Documentation topology

This branch adds one more diagnostics artifact. That is acceptable only if it reduces ambiguity. It becomes harmful if it creates another quasi-authoritative node that future work must reconcile.

This premortem must therefore remain a branch-specific risk note. It should not become a new process requirement, a replay framework, or a universal gate.

Preventive stance:

- cite this note for risk framing only;
- cite the actual packet or implementation evidence for slice authority;
- update queue/status files only when the active lane changes, not when a diagnostic opinion changes.

## Early-warning dashboard

Stop feature work and re-anchor if three or more of these appear in the same week:

- "clean-checkout replay" is used without naming the exact fixture and script;
- a claim cites this branch's fixture smoke for an artifact chain other than `execution_proxy_evidence`;
- an ignored `results/**` artifact is required to reproduce a decision-influencing claim;
- a research note drops its authority label or "does not authorize" boundary;
- RI/policy-router work uses exact-window evidence as runtime-candidate evidence without a fresh falsifier packet;
- paper/live or promotion language appears in replay-smoke discussion;
- env flags, cache policy, symbol mode, data-source policy, warmup, or config hash are omitted from a claim-bearing result;
- workers follow stale queue entries after newer slices have completed;
- a fixture-level test is described as historical-trace replay;
- config validation is confused with live-update authority;
- two agents touch overlapping surfaces without a shared scope contract;
- a branch SHA in a diagnostics note does not match the implementation-time checkout.

## Top preventive actions

1. Keep `execution_proxy_evidence` replay-smoke wording exact: one script, one tracked fixture, one bounded smoke.
2. Open separate carrier decisions for `edge_origin_isolation`, SCPE replay, or any other decision-influencing artifact chain.
3. Add explicit fixture-level versus historical-trace-level labels to future replay evidence notes.
4. Preserve "does not authorize" blocks in diagnostics and boundary packets.
5. Keep `next_phase_verkstad_queue` current when slices complete, so stale packets are not mistaken for live instructions.
6. Define the RI/policy-router transport/falsifier gate before any candidate-promotion language is allowed.
7. Treat paper/live isolation as its own seam check, not as a beneficiary of replay-smoke confidence.
8. Require claim headers for decision-influencing evidence: SHA, branch, clean/dirty status, input carrier, config, data policy, env flags, cache posture, and authority level.
9. Audit ignored-artifact dependencies before claiming reproducibility beyond a fixture.
10. Keep config-authority tests and runtime update policy separate from replay reproducibility.
11. Dispatch editor workers with explicit scope IN/OUT, base SHA, and forbidden inheritance boundaries.
12. Prefer one bounded risk-reduction slice at a time; do not convert this branch into a generic replay framework unless a later packet explicitly opens that scope.

## Hard stops

Stop immediately and reopen governance if any future step:

- treats this premortem as implementation authority;
- uses fixture-smoke evidence as full clean-checkout replay attestation;
- extends `execution_proxy_evidence` conclusions to other artifact chains without separate evidence;
- changes source, tests, fixtures, config, runtime behavior, paper/live paths, promotion semantics, champion surfaces, or family-rule surfaces by implication;
- relies on ignored local artifacts for a decision-influencing claim without marking the claim non-portable;
- turns local RI/policy-router evidence into runtime-candidate language without a transport/falsifier gate;
- cites replay or manifest evidence as paper/live readiness;
- updates runtime/default/config-authority behavior as a side effect of evidence-closeout work;
- continues from a stale packet instead of the current branch/queue state.

## What this note does not authorize

This note does not authorize:

- creating or modifying fixtures;
- changing `tests/**`;
- changing `scripts/**`;
- changing `src/**`;
- changing runtime config or config authority;
- tracking `results/research/**`;
- adding bundle/LFS policy;
- broad replay-smoke framework extraction;
- paper/live execution or dry-run acceptance;
- promotion, readiness, champion, or family-rule decisions;
- widening RI/policy-router runtime behavior.

## Bottom line

`feature/evidence-closeout-pilot` is most likely to fail by turning a precise reproducibility improvement into broad confidence. The branch's useful contribution is narrow: reduce one evidence dependency at a time, with exact carriers and bounded verification. Its main risk is not too little governance; it is imprecise inheritance. A tracked fixture smoke is valuable only while everyone remembers exactly what it proves and exactly what it does not prove.
